title: Callbacks
description: Pass a function so someone else can call it.

options: A function passed as an argument to be invoked later
questions.explanation: First-class functions make callbacks natural in JS.
run invokes cb with 5; the arrow doubles → 10.
setTimeout defers even with 0ms — 'b' runs after the current tick.
forEach invokes the callback for each element.

options: Deep nesting of callbacks that hurt readability
questions.explanation: The classic problem promises and async/await solve.

questions.prompt: Which is the CANONICAL Node-style callback signature?
questions.explanation: Node convention: error first, then value.
once returns a wrapper that only runs fn on the first call.
Passing a named function as a callback works the same as an inline arrow.
